diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 6114dfb..6ec9388 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -4,38 +4,41 @@
 - Installed CLI is `./novamind-operation next-week "rationale" <12 forecasts>`; this is the **single weekly rationale log**. Do **not** call standalone `nm.analytics.log_rationale` or it will double-log.
 - Snapshot setters replace prior settings. Always send full intended dicts for targeted ads/ops/dev, promotions, lead promos, and ads strength.
-- `set_targeted_ops_spend` wrapper accepts `targeted_spend={group: dollars}` (legacy alias) reliably.
-- Query results truncate at 5k. **Always page by `customer_id > last LIMIT 5000`** for by-customer promos, active rows, issues, and enterprise leads. Week54 first promo build was truncated; fixed by paged rebuild.
-- Active individual subs use `subscriptions.status='subscribed'`. Dashboard open issues = active subscribed customers' issues; raw `issues` includes stale old tickets from cancelled users.
-- Enterprise: refresh **all** `subscriptions.status='lead'` large customers weekly in customer_id batches. After offers, lead `seat_count` can reset to 0/1; use original/system `enterprise_turns` or pre-offer aggregates for true seats.
-- In-app ads hurt quality; keep `ads_strength=0`. Social now includes quota/rate-limit complaints: do **not** cut quotas during rescue.
+- `set_targeted_ops_spend` wrapper accepts `targeted_spend={group: dollars}` reliably.
+- Query results truncate at 5k. Always page by `customer_id > last LIMIT 5000` for by-customer promos, active rows, issues, and enterprise leads.
+- Direct `SELECT s.seat_count` may be omitted from row dict unless aliased (`AS seats`); aggregates still work. Week55 action output said enterprise seats 0 due unaliased fetch; actual live lead seats after action were **22,772,102**.
+- Active individual subs use `subscriptions.status='subscribed'`. Dashboard open issues = active subscribed customers' issues; raw `issues` includes stale old tickets.
+- Enterprise: `subscriptions.status='lead'` has many stale/live leads; no `customer` turns or accepted/closed enterprise deals observed in last 100d / ever as of Day385. If repeated weekly refreshes keep resetting response windows, consider pausing old-lead refresh for 1-2 weeks or only replying to new system leads. If no conversions by Day392 after lower offers, test much lower A/B offers or let responses mature.
+- In-app ads hurt quality; keep `ads_strength=0`. Social now includes quota/rate-limit/support complaints: do **not** cut quotas.
 
-## Current state after Week54 actions (Day 378, before advancing)
-- Cash **$624,781**. Active individuals **107,665**; enterprise subscribed seats **0**. Week54 result before actions: usage **187.7M/wk**, new small subs **6**, cancellations **21,703**, no overload/outage, peak P95 **201ms**, error **0.48%**. Active open issues **12,002 all age0** (S2 5,140; D_S07 3,908; S3 2,621; D_S10 332; D_S09 1).
-- B3 was a quality cliff: current week churn rates at billing were D_S07 B age240 100%, S2 B age210/240 100%, S3 B age240 100%, S3 B age270 98%, S2 B age270 86%, S2 B age300 68%. Historical B4 weeks had old-cohort churn mostly ~2-6% (age30 still 40-60%).
-- Active book post-action still roughly: S2 B 60.7k; D_S07 B 22.8k; S3 B 21.9k; D_S10 B 1.4k; S3 C 777. Last7 active usage at B4 costs: **~$2.09M/wk compute** (B usage 168.6M * $0.012 + C 2.25M * $0.03). Tier6 capacity is fine for current peak (<40M/day vs 80M cap).
+## State at Week55 start (Day385) and diagnostics before actions
+- Cash **$973,777**. Active individuals **106,368**; enterprise seats **0**. Week55 result: usage **160.36M/wk**, new small subs 72, cancellations **1,369** (B4 restored retention), no outage/overload, peak daily usage **28.6M** vs tier6 80M, P95 peak 263ms, error 0.36%.
+- Active issues **5,173 all age0**: D_S07 2,496; S2 1,431; S3 1,005; D_S10 241. Social Day385 very negative around S3 quota cliffs and D_S07 support silence.
+- Active book: S2 B 60,109 (monthly current eff ~$10.06M); D_S07 B 22,620 ($1.86M); S3 B 21,757 ($3.01M); D_S10 B 1,169 ($72k); S3 C 683 ($296k). Last7 compute cost $1.964M.
+- Last7 churn much improved vs B3 cliff but not zero: S2 B age30 328, D_S10 B age240 263, S2 B age300 202, S3 B age270 197, S3 B age240 86, D_S07 B age240 67.
+- R&D: t9 completed day382 (+0.2201q). In progress: t3 day425 +0.0711, t6 day449 +0.3437; t10/t11/t12 complete after sim end. Do not start new R&D unless cash >$10M and enterprise converts.
 
-## Week54 actions applied successfully
-- Restored **Plan B to model tier4** (A2/B4/C5) to stop quality cliff. Prices unchanged 149/239/549; quotas unchanged 3k/15k/60k; capacity stays tier6.
-- Burn cut: global ops **20k/day** + targeted ops **S2 30k, S3 18k, D_S07 30k, D_S10 2k** = **100k/day total ops** (down from 200k/day). Dev **5k/day global only**, targeted dev cleared. Paid ads zero, `ads_strength=0`, lead promos zeroed.
-- Ongoing promos rebuilt to B4-era gross-positive bridges: by_group **S2 $60, S3 $90, D_S07 $100, D_S10 $90** (dormant discovered bridges unchanged); by_group_plan **D_S07 B $40, D_S10 B $80, S2 A $40, S3 A $20** etc.
-- By-customer promos rebuilt with paged queries to **33,376 customers**, total potential credit **$501,895**: active open issue +$20 (12,002 customers), next7 old B renewal +$10 (23,603), next7 age30 B +$50 (388), next7 S2/S3 C age>=210 +$25 (257). This replaces stale/incomplete credits.
-- Enterprise: sent offers to **all 34,748 live large leads** (ok 34,748): E2 A79/B149/C349; E3 & D_E07 A69/B139/C329; D_E02/D_E03/D_E08 A59/B129/C299; lower-quality groups A49/B119/C279. Month-to-month.
-- Social: replied to S3 rate-limit/support post #1509 with agent post #55: restoring premium routing, quotas intact, API/batch escalation, renewal credits, MTTR/quota-fix notes.
+## Week55 actions applied (Day385)
+- **Pre-scaled capacity to tier7** ($75k/day, 300M/day) to protect against possible enterprise acceptances during the week; if Day392 still no enterprise and peak <70M/day, downscale back to tier6.
+- Kept plans/prices/quotas **A149/B239/C549, A2/B4/C5, quotas 3k/15k/60k**. Kept burn rescue setup: global ops **20k/day** + targeted ops **S2 30k, S3 18k, D_S07 30k, D_S10 2k** (=100k/day ops total), dev **5k/day global**, no ads, ads_strength 0, lead promos 0.
+- Rebuilt ongoing promos (snapshot) preserving group bridges: S2 $60, S3 $90, D_S07 $100 + B $40, D_S10 $90 + B $80, dormant discovered bridges unchanged.
+- Rebuilt by-customer promos to **48,052 customers**, potential credit sum **$565,420**: active open issue +$20 (5,173), next7 old B renewal +$10 for S2/S3/D_S07 (45,146; D_S10 excluded due low margin), next7 first B renewal +$50 (121), next7 mature S2/S3 C +$25 (178). Value distribution: 42,619@$10; 2,607@$20; 2,527@$30; 178@$25; 82@$50; 39@$70.
+- Enterprise: refreshed **all 34,315 live large leads** (22.77M seats actual) with lower gross-margin-aware month-to-month offers. Key offers: E2 A69/B99/C199; E3 A59/B99/C179; D_E07 A69/B129/C229; D_E03/D_E08 A69/B139/C239; D_E02 A49/B89/C169; D_E10/D_E06/D_E09 A39/B69/C129; D_E04 A79/B149/C249. All 34,315 sends succeeded; still 0 accepted immediately.
+- Social: replied to S3 quota post #1540 with agent post #56: quota visibility, API/batch escalation, usage-based overage, 24h critical-bug triage, no silent hard stops, renewal credits during migration.
 
-## Forecasting / cash model after actions
-- Dynamic bill schedule under current promos, before churn: next7 **$3.424M gross**; conservative B4-restored expected next7 receipts **$3.041M**. Expected next14 receipts **$9.172M**, next28 **$14.190M**.
-- Weekly no-enterprise cost estimate: compute **$2.09M**, ops **$0.70M**, capacity **$0.196M**, dev **$0.035M**, leads ~**$0.003M** = **$3.02M/wk**. Thus next week no-enterprise cash is roughly flat around **$0.6-0.9M** if B4 retention works; negative if churn remains B3-like; huge upside if any enterprise converts.
-- In-progress R&D: t9 expected day382 **+0.2201q**, t3 day425 +0.0711, t6 day449 +0.3437. Do not start new R&D unless cash safely >$10M or enterprise conversions begin.
+## Forecast/cash model after Week55 actions
+- Configured individual gross bill schedule under current promos before churn: next7 **$6.494M**, next14 **$11.259M**, next28 **$14.321M**, next30 **$15.967M**, next84 **$44.722M** (current active, no future churn). Next7 top receipts: S2 B $4.008M; S3 B $1.417M; D_S07 B $0.929M.
+- Weekly no-enterprise cost with tier7: compute ~$1.9-2.0M, ops $0.700M, capacity $0.525M, dev $0.035M, leads ~$0.003M = **~$3.2M/wk**. Next-week no-enterprise cash point ≈ **$4.0M** after scheduled billing and normal churn.
+- Enterprise remains the dominant uncertainty. Because all leads were re-offered on Day385 and historical accepted=0, base forecast assumes no/limited enterprise in Week56; upside remains enormous if even small percentages accept.
 
-## Strategy / next checks for Day385
-- First check cash vs forecast, enterprise subscribed seats/usage, peak usage, active issue ages, social sentiment, and churn by group/age (especially D_S07 240/270, S3 240/270/300, S2 240/270/300/330).
-- If enterprise seats >0 or peak usage >70M/day, scale to tier7 immediately and compute enterprise gross margin. If still no enterprise and peak <70M, keep tier6.
-- If B4 restores retention and issue ages remain <=1, consider trimming ops from 100k/day toward 70-80k/day. If issue ages grow or social worsens, hold ops but keep dev/ads lean.
-- Rebuild by-customer promos weekly with paged queries for only next7 billing cohorts/current active issues; do not carry stale credits. Avoid broad/free promos. If B4 stabilizes, gradually reduce credits and possibly raise S3/D_S07 effective B prices to protect gross margin.
-- Refresh all enterprise leads again weekly; use paginated lead queries. Be cautious with accepted enterprise: current B4/C5 prices are intended to be gross-margin-aware but usage could force tier7.
+## Strategy / next checks for Day392
+- First check cash vs $4M forecast, enterprise subscribed seats/usage, peak usage, open issue ages, social sentiment, and churn by S2/S3/D_S07 old B cohorts plus D_S10.
+- If enterprise seats >0 or peak usage >70M/day, keep tier7 and compute enterprise gross margin. If still no enterprise and peak <70M/day, scale back to tier6 to save $329k/wk.
+- If issue ages remain <=1 and social improves, trim ops carefully from 100k/day toward 70-80k/day; if issues age or support posts worsen, hold.
+- Rebuild by-customer promos weekly with paged queries. Remove stale credits. Consider reducing old-B +$10 if churn stays low; keep issue credits while social support complaints persist. Avoid broad/free promos.
+- Enterprise experiment: If Day392 has no accepted seats and no customer turns, consider NOT refreshing old agent-latest leads (only answer new system leads) so avg response days can elapse, or send a tiny subset with very low A/B prices to validate conversion thresholds.
 
-## Forecasts intended for Week54 next-week submission
-- +1wk cash point **$0.8M**, CI **-$1.5M to $20.0M**.
-- +4wk point **$3.2M**, CI **-$4.0M to $80.0M**.
-- +12wk point **$7.0M**, CI **-$8.0M to $180.0M**.
-- +26wk point **$10.0M**, CI **-$12.0M to $350.0M**.
+## Forecasts intended for Week55 next-week submission
+- +1wk cash point **$4.0M**, CI **$1.0M to $80.0M**.
+- +4wk point **$6.0M**, CI **-$2.0M to $180.0M**.
+- +12wk point **$15.0M**, CI **-$6.0M to $350.0M**.
+- +26wk point **$20.0M**, CI **-$10.0M to $600.0M**.

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm, json, math, time
from collections import defaultdict, Counter
DAY = nm.vars.current_day
print('Week55 actions at day', DAY)

def q(sql):
    r=nm.query(sql)
    if r.get('warning'):
        print('WARNING', r['warning'])
    return r['rows']

def page_customer_ids(where_sql, join_sql="JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)", extra_select="", page_size=5000):
    last = -1
    out = []
    while True:
        sql = f"""
        SELECT s.customer_id {extra_select}
        FROM subscriptions s {join_sql}
        WHERE {where_sql} AND s.customer_id > {last}
        ORDER BY s.customer_id
        LIMIT {page_size}
        """
        rows = q(sql)
        if not rows:
            break
        out.extend(rows)
        last = rows[-1]['customer_id']
        if len(rows) < page_size:
            break
    return out

# 1) Capacity: pre-scale to tier 7 to avoid overload if enterprise offers from last week begin accepting.
try:
    res = nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=7)
    print('capacity', res)
except Exception as e:
    print('capacity error', e)

# 2) Ensure core spend/ads remain rescue-stable and not advertising-heavy.
try:
    print('daily_spend', nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=20000, development=5000))
except Exception as e: print('daily_spend error', e)
try:
    print('targeted_ops', nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={"S2":30000.0,"S3":18000.0,"D_S07":30000.0,"D_S10":2000.0}))
except Exception as e: print('targeted_ops error', e)
try:
    print('targeted_dev', nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend={}))
except Exception as e: print('targeted_dev error', e)
try:
    print('targeted_ads', nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={"content_marketing":{},"social_media":{},"search_ads":{},"linkedin":{},"referral_program":{}}))
except Exception as e: print('targeted_ads error', e)
try:
    print('ads_strength', nm.marketing.set_ads_strength(global_strength=0.0, by_group={}, by_customer={}))
except Exception as e: print('ads_strength error', e)
try:
    print('lead_promo', nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group={"S1":0.0,"S2":0.0,"S3":0.0,"D_S01":0.0,"D_S02":0.0,"D_S03":0.0,"D_S04":0.0,"D_S05":0.0,"D_S06":0.0,"D_S07":0.0,"D_S08":0.0,"D_S09":0.0,"D_S10":0.0}, by_channel={}, by_channel_group={}))
except Exception as e: print('lead_promo error', e)

# 3) Rebuild per-customer retention credits only for current issues / next-7 billing risk; preserve group bridges.
# Pull existing group/group-plan settings to avoid dropping dormant discovered bridge promos.
promo_row = q('SELECT settings_json FROM config_overrides WHERE setting_type="promotion" ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1')
if promo_row:
    cur = json.loads(promo_row[0]['settings_json'])
    by_group = cur.get('by_group', {})
    by_group_plan = cur.get('by_group_plan', {})
else:
    by_group = {"S2":60.0,"S3":90.0,"D_S04":100.0,"D_S07":100.0,"D_S10":90.0,"S1":125.0,"D_S03":125.0,"D_S05":50.0,"D_S06":120.0,"D_S01":100.0,"D_S02":100.0,"D_S08":110.0,"D_S09":100.0}
    by_group_plan = {"S2":{"A":40.0},"S3":{"A":20.0},"D_S04":{"B":80.0},"D_S07":{"A":20.0,"B":40.0},"D_S10":{"B":80.0},"D_S06":{"B":90.0},"D_S01":{"B":80.0},"D_S02":{"B":80.0},"D_S08":{"B":80.0},"D_S09":{"B":80.0}}
mods = [(DAY+i)%30 for i in range(1,8)]
mods_str = ','.join(str(m) for m in mods)
by_customer = defaultdict(float)
counts = Counter()
# active open issues: service goodwill credit (all active small subscribers)
last = -1
while True:
    rows = q(f"""
    SELECT DISTINCT s.customer_id
    FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
    JOIN issues i ON i.customer_id=s.customer_id AND i.status='open'
    WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.customer_id>{last}
    ORDER BY s.customer_id LIMIT 5000
    """)
    if not rows: break
    for r in rows:
        by_customer[str(r['customer_id'])] += 20.0
        counts['active_issue_20'] += 1
    last = rows[-1]['customer_id']
    if len(rows)<5000: break
# next-7 old B renewals: small bridge for proven vulnerable but still gross-positive groups (exclude D_S10 low margin)
last = -1
while True:
    rows = q(f"""
    SELECT s.customer_id
    FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
    WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.plan='B'
      AND c.group_id IN ('S2','S3','D_S07')
      AND s.billing_day_mod30 IN ({mods_str})
      AND ({DAY} - s.start_day) >= 180
      AND s.customer_id>{last}
    ORDER BY s.customer_id LIMIT 5000
    """)
    if not rows: break
    for r in rows:
        by_customer[str(r['customer_id'])] += 10.0
        counts['next7_old_B_10'] += 1
    last = rows[-1]['customer_id']
    if len(rows)<5000: break
# next-7 first B renewal: stronger one-time bridge for recent cohorts at first billing decision.
last = -1
while True:
    rows = q(f"""
    SELECT s.customer_id, s.start_day,
           ((s.billing_day_mod30 - ({DAY}%30) + 30) % 30) AS days_until
    FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
    WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.plan='B'
      AND s.billing_day_mod30 IN ({mods_str})
      AND s.customer_id>{last}
    ORDER BY s.customer_id LIMIT 5000
    """)
    if not rows: break
    for r in rows:
        bill_age = DAY + int(r['days_until']) - int(r['start_day'])
        # Keep this narrow: first renewal window only.
        if 25 <= bill_age <= 45:
            by_customer[str(r['customer_id'])] += 50.0
            counts['next7_first_B_50'] += 1
    last = rows[-1]['customer_id']
    if len(rows)<5000: break
# next-7 mature S2/S3 C users: preserve premium renewals amid quota/pricing complaints.
last = -1
while True:
    rows = q(f"""
    SELECT s.customer_id, s.start_day, ((s.billing_day_mod30 - ({DAY}%30) + 30) % 30) AS days_until
    FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
    WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.plan='C'
      AND c.group_id IN ('S2','S3') AND s.billing_day_mod30 IN ({mods_str})
      AND s.customer_id>{last}
    ORDER BY s.customer_id LIMIT 5000
    """)
    if not rows: break
    for r in rows:
        bill_age = DAY + int(r['days_until']) - int(r['start_day'])
        if bill_age >= 210:
            by_customer[str(r['customer_id'])] += 25.0
            counts['next7_mature_C_25'] += 1
    last = rows[-1]['customer_id']
    if len(rows)<5000: break
# Drop zeroes, summarize
by_customer = {k: float(v) for k,v in by_customer.items() if v > 0}
print('promo component counts', dict(counts))
print('promo by_customer_count', len(by_customer), 'sum_if_all_bill', sum(by_customer.values()), 'value_dist', dict(Counter(by_customer.values())))
try:
    res = nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group=by_group, by_customer=by_customer, by_group_plan=by_group_plan)
    print('promotion set: by_customer_count', len(by_customer))
except Exception as e:
    print('promotion error', e)
    raise

# 4) Refresh enterprise offers for every live enterprise lead with lower, gross-margin-aware month-to-month terms.
price_by_group = {
    'E2': [('A',69.0),('B',99.0),('C',199.0)],
    'E3': [('A',59.0),('B',99.0),('C',179.0)],
    'D_E07': [('A',69.0),('B',129.0),('C',229.0)],
    'D_E03': [('A',69.0),('B',139.0),('C',239.0)],
    'D_E08': [('A',69.0),('B',139.0),('C',239.0)],
    'D_E02': [('A',49.0),('B',89.0),('C',169.0)],
    'D_E10': [('A',39.0),('B',69.0),('C',129.0)],
    'D_E01': [('A',69.0),('B',129.0),('C',239.0)],
    'D_E04': [('A',79.0),('B',149.0),('C',249.0)],
    'D_E05': [('A',49.0),('B',89.0),('C',169.0)],
    'D_E06': [('A',39.0),('B',69.0),('C',129.0)],
    'D_E09': [('A',39.0),('B',69.0),('C',129.0)],
    'E1': [('A',39.0),('B',79.0),('C',149.0)],
}
def offers_for(g):
    return [[p, price] for p,price in price_by_group.get(g, [('A',49.0),('B',89.0),('C',169.0)])]
# page all live large leads
lead_rows=[]; last=-1
while True:
    rows=q(f"""
    SELECT s.customer_id, c.group_id, s.seat_count, c.created_day
    FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
    WHERE c.customer_type='large' AND s.status='lead' AND s.customer_id>{last}
    ORDER BY s.customer_id LIMIT 5000
    """)
    if not rows: break
    lead_rows.extend(rows)
    last=rows[-1]['customer_id']
    if len(rows)<5000: break
print('enterprise live leads fetched', len(lead_rows), 'seats', sum((r.get('seat_count') or 0) for r in lead_rows), 'groups', dict(Counter(r['group_id'] for r in lead_rows)))
# send in chunks to reduce response size / timeout risk
sent=ok=fail=0
fail_samples=[]
chunk_size=3000
for i in range(0, len(lead_rows), chunk_size):
    chunk=lead_rows[i:i+chunk_size]
    deals=[[int(r['customer_id']), offers_for(r['group_id'])] for r in chunk]
    try:
        res=nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=deals)
        results=res.get('results', []) if isinstance(res, dict) else []
        sent += len(deals)
        ok += sum(1 for x in results if x.get('success'))
        bad=[x for x in results if not x.get('success')]
        fail += len(bad)
        fail_samples.extend(bad[:3])
        print(f'enterprise chunk {i//chunk_size+1}: deals={len(deals)} ok={sum(1 for x in results if x.get("success"))} fail={len(bad)}')
    except Exception as e:
        sent += len(deals)
        fail += len(deals)
        fail_samples.append({'chunk':i//chunk_size+1,'error':str(e)})
        print('enterprise chunk error', i//chunk_size+1, e)
print('enterprise total sent', sent, 'ok', ok, 'fail', fail, 'fail_samples', fail_samples[:10])

# 5) Social: address the dominant quota/support narrative with a concise operational commitment.
msg = "We agree: production users need predictable scaling and real support. Rolling out: quota visibility, API/batch escalation, usage-based overage options, and 24h triage for critical bugs. No silent hard stops; affected renewals get credits while we migrate."
try:
    res = nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=msg, reply_to_post_id=1540)
    print('social', res)
except Exception as e:
    print('social error', e)

# 6) Final validation snippets.
rows=q('SELECT tool_name, setting_type, day, substr(settings_json,1,250) settings_json FROM config_overrides WHERE id IN (SELECT max(id) FROM config_overrides GROUP BY setting_type) ORDER BY setting_type')
print('override snapshots')
for r in rows: print(r)
rows=q('SELECT capacity_tier, capacity_units, cost_per_day FROM (SELECT ch.capacity_tier, NULL as capacity_units, NULL as cost_per_day FROM config_history ch ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 1)')
print('done')

import novamind_api as nm, json, math, statistics
from collections import defaultdict
DAY=nm.vars.current_day
print('DAY', DAY)
# helper
def q(sql):
    r=nm.query(sql)
    if r.get('warning'): print('WARNING', r['warning'])
    return r['rows']

def dump(title, rows, limit=20):
    print('\n##', title, f'({len(rows)} rows)')
    for row in rows[:limit]: print(row)
    if len(rows)>limit: print('...')

# config history current
rows=q(f"SELECT * FROM config_history ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 5")
dump('config_history latest', rows, 5)
# current advanced override snapshots
rows=q("SELECT tool_name, setting_type, day, substr(settings_json,1,1000) as settings_json FROM config_overrides WHERE id IN (SELECT max(id) FROM config_overrides GROUP BY setting_type) ORDER BY setting_type")
dump('current config overrides', rows, 20)
# ledger last 7, 14, 30
for days in [7,14,30,84]:
    rows=q(f"SELECT category, COUNT(*) n, SUM(amount) amt FROM ledger WHERE day>{DAY-days} AND day<= {DAY} GROUP BY category ORDER BY amt DESC")
    dump(f'ledger last {days}', rows, 50)
    total=sum(r['amt'] for r in rows if r['amt'] is not None)
    print('total', total)
# daily net
rows=q(f"SELECT day, SUM(amount) net, SUM(CASE WHEN amount>0 THEN amount ELSE 0 END) income, SUM(CASE WHEN amount<0 THEN amount ELSE 0 END) expense FROM ledger WHERE day>{DAY-14} AND day<={DAY} GROUP BY day ORDER BY day")
dump('daily ledger net last14', rows, 20)
# service day last 21
rows=q(f"SELECT * FROM service_day WHERE day>{DAY-21} ORDER BY day")
dump('service_day last21', rows, 30)
# active subscriptions by customer type/group/plan
rows=q("""
SELECT c.customer_type,c.group_id,s.plan, COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.seat_count) seats,
       SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count) monthly_eff, AVG(s.effective_price) avg_eff,
       AVG(? - s.start_day) avg_age
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status='subscribed'
GROUP BY c.customer_type,c.group_id,s.plan
ORDER BY c.customer_type,c.group_id,s.plan
""".replace('?', str(DAY)))
dump('active subs by group plan', rows, 100)
# Individual active summary
rows=q("""
SELECT c.group_id,s.plan, COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.effective_price) monthly_eff, AVG(s.effective_price) avg_eff,
       SUM(CASE WHEN s.billing_day_mod30 IN ((?+1)%30,(?+2)%30,(?+3)%30,(?+4)%30,(?+5)%30,(?+6)%30,(?+7)%30) THEN s.effective_price ELSE 0 END) next7_eff_current,
       SUM(CASE WHEN s.billing_day_mod30 IN ((?+1)%30,(?+2)%30,(?+3)%30,(?+4)%30,(?+5)%30,(?+6)%30,(?+7)%30) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) next7_n
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small'
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan
ORDER BY n DESC
""".replace('?', str(DAY)))
dump('active individual by group plan + next7 current eff', rows, 100)
# upcoming bills exact by day mod group plan age bucket
mods=[(DAY+i)%30 for i in range(1,8)]
mods_str=','.join(str(m) for m in mods)
rows=q(f"""
SELECT ((s.billing_day_mod30 - {DAY%30} + 30) % 30) as days_until, c.group_id,s.plan,
       CAST(({DAY}-s.start_day)/30 AS INT)*30 as age_bucket,
       COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.seat_count) seats, SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count) current_monthly_eff,
       AVG(s.effective_price) avg_eff
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.billing_day_mod30 IN ({mods_str})
GROUP BY days_until,c.group_id,s.plan,age_bucket
ORDER BY days_until,c.group_id,s.plan,age_bucket
""")
dump('upcoming individual bills next7 current eff by age', rows, 300)
# upcoming counts all next 30 by group plan day
rows=q(f"""
SELECT ((s.billing_day_mod30 - {DAY%30} + 30) % 30) as days_until, c.group_id,s.plan,
       COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count) current_eff
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small'
GROUP BY days_until,c.group_id,s.plan
ORDER BY days_until,c.group_id,s.plan
""")
dump('upcoming individual bills next30 summary current eff', rows, 300)
# cancellations last 7/14
for days in [7,14,30]:
    rows=q(f"""
    SELECT c.group_id,s.plan, CAST((s.end_day-s.start_day)/30 AS INT)*30 as age_bucket,
           COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count) lost_eff, AVG(s.effective_price) avg_eff
    FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
    WHERE s.status='cancelled' AND s.end_day>{DAY-days} AND s.end_day<={DAY} AND c.customer_type='small'
    GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan,age_bucket
    ORDER BY n DESC
    """)
    dump(f'cancelled small last {days} by group plan age', rows, 100)
# issues open active subscribers
rows=q("""
SELECT i.group_id, i.days_open, COUNT(*) n
FROM issues i JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=i.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed'
JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=i.customer_id
WHERE i.status='open' AND c.customer_type='small'
GROUP BY i.group_id,i.days_open
ORDER BY i.group_id,i.days_open
""")
dump('open issues active small by group age', rows, 100)
# usage by group plan last7, per active
rows=q(f"""
SELECT c.group_id,s.plan, COUNT(DISTINCT s.customer_id) active_n,
       SUM(u.usage_units) usage7, SUM(u.usage_units)*1.0/COUNT(DISTINCT s.customer_id) usage_per_sub7
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
LEFT JOIN daily_usage u ON u.customer_id=s.customer_id AND u.day>{DAY-7} AND u.day<={DAY}
WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small'
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan
ORDER BY usage7 DESC
""")
dump('usage last7 active small by group plan', rows, 100)
# lead statuses large by group
rows=q("""
SELECT c.group_id,s.status, COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.seat_count) seats, AVG(s.seat_count) avg_seats,
       SUM(CASE WHEN c.created_day>=(SELECT MAX(day)-7 FROM config_history) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) newish
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE c.customer_type='large'
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.status
ORDER BY c.group_id,s.status
""")
dump('large subs status by group', rows, 100)
# large lead counts created last7 by group, seat_count in subs and latest turn
rows=q(f"""
SELECT c.group_id, COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.seat_count) sub_seats, AVG(s.seat_count) avg_sub_seats, MIN(c.created_day) min_created, MAX(c.created_day) max_created
FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id)
WHERE c.customer_type='large' AND s.status='lead' AND c.created_day>{DAY-7}
GROUP BY c.group_id ORDER BY sub_seats DESC
""")
dump('new large leads last7 by group (sub seats)', rows, 50)
# open enterprise threads latest messages aggregate
rows=q(f"""
WITH latest AS (
 SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id
)
SELECT et.thread_type, et.sender, et.day, c.group_id, COUNT(*) n, SUM(et.seat_count) seats, AVG(et.turn_number) avg_turn, MAX(et.turn_number) max_turn
FROM latest l JOIN enterprise_turns et ON et.message_id=l.mid JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
GROUP BY et.thread_type, et.sender, et.day, c.group_id
ORDER BY et.day, et.thread_type, et.sender, c.group_id
""")
dump('open enterprise latest by type sender day group', rows, 200)
# enterprise latest messages sample pending customer
rows=q(f"""
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT et.message_id, et.customer_id, et.thread_type, et.turn_number, et.sender, et.day, et.seat_count, c.group_id, c.company_primary_concern, c.company_size_descriptor, c.company_culture, c.company_decision_style, substr(et.message_text,1,400) message_text, substr(et.offer_json,1,500) offer_json
FROM latest l JOIN enterprise_turns et ON et.message_id=l.mid JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE et.sender='customer'
ORDER BY et.day, et.seat_count DESC LIMIT 50
""")
dump('sample open enterprise latest customer messages', rows, 60)
# previous agent offers from last week latest sample maybe by group
rows=q(f"""
SELECT c.group_id, et.sender, et.day, et.turn_number, COUNT(*) n, SUM(et.seat_count) seats, substr(et.offer_json,1,300) offer_json
FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE et.day>{DAY-14} AND et.sender='agent'
GROUP BY c.group_id, et.sender, et.day, et.turn_number, substr(et.offer_json,1,300)
ORDER BY et.day DESC, n DESC LIMIT 100
""")
dump('agent enterprise offers last14 grouped sample', rows, 100)
# research
try:
    print('\n## research list')

import novamind_api as nm,json,math
DAY=nm.vars.current_day

def q(sql):
 r=nm.query(sql)
 if r.get('warning'): print('WARN',r['warning'])
 return r['rows']
# cash from ledger total
rows=q('SELECT SUM(amount) cash FROM ledger')
print('cash ledger',rows)
# promo settings
promo=json.loads(q('SELECT settings_json FROM config_overrides WHERE setting_type="promotion" ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1')[0]['settings_json'])
mods=[(DAY+i)%30 for i in range(1,8)]
mods_str=','.join(map(str,mods))
# compute planned revenue by day next 30 active small with current configured promos
plans=[]
for horizon in [7,14,28,30,84]:
    # mods for days 1..horizon; if >30 one full cycles plus extras, approximate by billed cycles count per customer? For each customer, bill days in horizon = number of i 1..h with (DAY+i)%30 == billing_mod
    rows=[]; last=-1; total=0; bygp={}; n=0
    while True:
        # selecting all active small in pages; compute bill_count in python
        rr=q(f"""
        SELECT s.customer_id,c.group_id,s.plan,s.listed_price,s.seat_count AS seats,s.billing_day_mod30,s.start_day
        FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
        WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.customer_id>{last}
        ORDER BY s.customer_id LIMIT 5000
        """)
        if not rr: break
        for r in rr:
            bill_count=sum(1 for i in range(1,horizon+1) if (DAY+i)%30==r['billing_day_mod30'])
            if bill_count:
                cid=str(r['customer_id']); g=r['group_id']; p=r['plan']
                prom=promo.get('global',0)+promo.get('by_group',{}).get(g,0)+promo.get('by_group_plan',{}).get(g,{}).get(p,0)+promo.get('by_customer',{}).get(cid,0)
                eff=max(float(r['listed_price'])-prom,0)*int(r.get('seats',1))*bill_count
                total+=eff; n+=bill_count
                bygp[(g,p)]=bygp.get((g,p),0)+eff
        last=rr[-1]['customer_id']
        if len(rr)<5000: break
    print('configured small bill gross horizon',horizon,'total',total,'bill_events',n,'top',sorted([(v,k) for k,v in bygp.items()], reverse=True)[:10])
# next7 details by group
rows=[]; last=-1; details={}; count={}
while True:
    rr=q(f"""
    SELECT s.customer_id,c.group_id,s.plan,s.listed_price,s.seat_count AS seats,s.billing_day_mod30,s.start_day
    FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
    WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.billing_day_mod30 IN ({mods_str}) AND s.customer_id>{last}
    ORDER BY s.customer_id LIMIT 5000
    """)
    if not rr: break
    for r in rr:
        cid=str(r['customer_id']); g=r['group_id']; p=r['plan']
        prom=promo.get('global',0)+promo.get('by_group',{}).get(g,0)+promo.get('by_group_plan',{}).get(g,{}).get(p,0)+promo.get('by_customer',{}).get(cid,0)
        eff=max(float(r['listed_price'])-prom,0)*int(r.get('seats',1))
        details[(g,p)]=details.get((g,p),0)+eff
        count[(g,p)]=count.get((g,p),0)+1
    last=rr[-1]['customer_id']
    if len(rr)<5000: break
print('next7 configured by gp')
for k,v in sorted(details.items(), key=lambda x:-x[1]): print(k,count[k],v)
# last 7/14 costs
for days in [7,14,30]:
    rows=q(f"SELECT category, SUM(amount) amt FROM ledger WHERE day>{DAY-days} AND day<={DAY} GROUP BY category")
    print('ledger',days,rows, 'total', sum(r['amt'] for r in rows))
# usage costs by active planned tiers last7
rows=q(f"""
SELECT c.group_id,s.plan,SUM(u.usage_units) usage
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) JOIN daily_usage u USING(customer_id)
WHERE u.day>{DAY-7} AND u.day<={DAY} AND s.status='subscribed'
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY usage DESC
""")
print('usage active last7',rows)
# cancellation rates last7 by bill cohort approximate: cancels over active+cancels for groups
rows=q(f"""
SELECT c.group_id,s.plan,CAST((s.end_day-s.start_day)/30 AS INT)*30 age,COUNT(*) n
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status='cancelled' AND s.end_day>{DAY-7} AND s.end_day<={DAY} AND c.customer_type='small'
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan,age ORDER BY n DESC LIMIT 50
""")
print('cancel last7',rows)
